streams.pump_role: Single premixed feed containing all reagents in DES
streams.reasoning: From Chemistry Plan stream_logic: All reagents premixed in single stream since reaction only proceeds at elevated temperature. DES viscosity requires single pump approach. Concentration reduced from batch 0.5 M to 0.067 M to match calculated reactor concentration.

mixing_order_reasoning: From Chemistry Plan: Single premixed feed eliminates need for mixing. All reagents stable together at room temperature since cycloaddition only initiates at 120°C.
chemistry_notes: Thermal 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition proceeding via in-situ nitrile oxide generation from ethyl nitroacetate at 120°C, followed by [3+2] cycloaddition with phenylacetylene. Key design decisions: (1) Single premixed feed to handle viscous DES efficiently, (2) 60 min residence time from analogy scaling to ensure complete nitrile oxide formation, (3) 5 bar BPR required to prevent DES component volatilization at 120°C, (4) PFA tubing for chemical compatibility with DES at high temperature.

reasoning_per_field.residence_time_min: Analogy 3 used 20 min for [3+2] cycloaddition at 60°C. Our reaction at 120°C with nitrile oxide generation step requires longer time. Calculation estimated 60 min range [1-90 min]. Using 60 min from calculation upper bound to ensure complete conversion.
reasoning_per_field.temperature_C: Same as batch 120°C - required for nitrile oxide generation from ethyl nitroacetate. No adjustment needed.
reasoning_per_field.BPR_bar: Calculation: EG bp=197°C, op=120°C, but gas generation (H2O loss) detected - BPR mandatory at 5.0 bar to maintain liquid phase and prevent uncontrolled degassing.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_material: PFA for high temperature (120°C) and chemical compatibility with DES components (TBAB/EG). Not photochemistry so transparency not required.
reasoning_per_field.concentration_M: Batch used 0.5 M. Calculation shows reactor concentration 0.067 M due to dilution effect in flow. Using calculated value.
reasoning_per_field.flow_rate_mL_min: Calculation: V_R=30 mL, τ=60 min gives Q=0.5 mL/min. Verified against analogies - Analogy 2 used 0.12 mL/min for similar thermal process.
reasoning_per_field.reactor_volume_mL: Calculation: 30 mL from τ×Q = 60×0.5. Analogy 1 used 30 mL steel coil for high temperature - directly applicable.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_ID_mm: Calculation: 1.0 mm gives Re=10.6 (laminar), Pe=691 (plug flow). Analogy thermal processes typically use 1-2 mm ID for good heat transfer.
